what you said there's no no good evidence of derivative information throwing back this that is these error gradients flowing backwards. Obviously the brain has top down connections if you look at the perceptual system there's a forward direction that goes from the thalamus up to infr temp cortex where you recognize things and. The thalamus is are where the input comes in from the eyes and. There's Connections in the backward Direction. But the connections in the backward Direction don't look at all like what you'd need for back propagation for example in two cortical areas the connections coming back don't go to the same cells as connections going forward come from it's not reciprocal in that sense.
yeah there's a loop between the cortical areas but information in one cortical area goes through about six different neurons before it gets back to where it started. So it's a loop it's not it's not like a miror system.
but my question is you talk about turning the static image into a boring video that allows you to have top down effects that's right. So you have to think of there being a forward Direction which is going from lower layers to higher layers and then orthogonal to that was the time dimension.
and so if I have a video even if it's a video of just a single thing that stays still I can be going up and down through the layers as I go forwards in time.
